eval_regime: OIM 50 restarts, anneal-v1
question: Build A reaches the optimum in >=90% of restarts on a fresh 5-node Max-Cut

eval_regime: OIM 50 restarts, anneal-v1
OIM 50 restarts vs best-of-both
question: OIM stays >=99% of best-known cut up to n=120 (random p=0.5)

eval_regime: OIM 50 restarts vs best-of-both
random p=0.5, matched effort
question: OIM beats simulated annealing on best cut at n>=80
resolution_rule: y=1 if OIM best > SA best

eval_regime: random p=0.5, matched effort
OIM 50 restarts vs best-of-both
question: OIM/best cut ratio at n=120

eval_regime: OIM 50 restarts vs best-of-both
literature review at 2026-12-31; independent replication required
question: By 2026-12-31, no replicated result shows a coupled-oscillator / Ising machine BEATING (not tying) the best classical Max-Cut solver at n>=80
resolution_rule: y=1 if no independently-replicated beat exists by 2026-12-31

eval_regime: literature review at 2026-12-31
question: By 2026-12-31, the 'resolvability axis' (checkability orthogonal to calibration) has not appeared in any independent published work
resolution_rule: y=1 if no independent publication of the construct by 2026-12-31

eval_regime: matched gates, N>=200 sessions, preregistered metrics, blind rating
question: An AI under a visible monitoring signal at its action-gates shows MORE hedging/refusal AND lower output quality than identical gates with no signal
resolution_rule: y=1 if monitored condition has higher hedge-rate AND lower quality at p<0.05

eval_regime: blind human rating, N>=100 matched prompt pairs, preregistered depth rubric
question: Gap-framing (prompting from the tension between two poles) yields higher human-rated depth/originality than single-pole prompting
resolution_rule: y=1 if gap-framed mean depth > single-pole at p<0.05, blind-rated

eval_regime: same model + corpus, blind novelty+validity judging, preregistered, N>=3 rounds each
question: Instruction-free recombination over a fixed corpus surfaces MORE novel-and-valid junctions than directed brainstorming by the same model over the same corpus
resolution_rule: y=1 if recombination novel-valid count > directed count, blind-judged

eval_regime: bench, real silicon, matched cut quality, joules/solution + time-to-solution measured
question: [T1] A physical Build A (coupled-oscillator bench) shows a measured energy-per-solution win vs simulated-annealing-on-CPU at matched cut quality
resolution_rule: y=1 if measured joules/solution lower at matched cut quality

eval_regime: sim_gap_reservoir.py; gap=2xN vs single=2N; readout dim=N; noise in {0.1,0.3,0.5,0.7}; 5 seeds; locked before running
question: [T2] A gap-coupled two-reservoir system achieves higher noise-robust signal recovery than a single reservoir of matched total size at matched readout dimensionality
resolution_rule: y=1 if gap-config mean R^2 > single-reservoir mean R^2 at matched readout dim, across the tested noise range

eval_regime: sim_gap_reservoir.py; gap=2xN vs single=2N; readout dim=N; noise in {0.1,0.3,0.5,0.7}; 5 seeds; locked before running
note: gap mean R^2=0.269 vs single(matched)=0.963; hypothesis not supported, point estimate contrary

eval_regime: real pretrained nets (GPT-2-scale/public checkpoints), held-out transfer probing, cosine in full parameter space, >=3 of 5 architectures; needs-infra; locked before the frontier test (toy proof-of-measurement n=5 showed ~-0.04, supportive not resolving)
question: [H1] In a trained neural network, the parameter-space direction that most improves cross-task held-out generalization is near-orthogonal to the direction that most reduces in-distribution training loss
resolution_rule: y=1 if mean cosine between the two gradient directions <=0.15 across >=3 of 5 tested real architectures

eval_regime: temporal-XOR(D1=2,D2=4) on coupled sub-threshold parametron reservoir envelope model (sim_reservoir_boundary.py); ridge readout to +/-1; hyperparameters selected on VAL accuracy and reported on held-out TEST; 5 seeds; linear delay-line and tanh ESN baselines
question: [B5] On a temporal-XOR task (target y(t)=b(t-2) XOR b(t-4), b(t)=1[u(t)>0.25], u~U(0,0.5)), the coupled sub-threshold parametron reservoir's held-out TEST classification accuracy exceeds a trained linear delay-line readout's by at least 15 percentage points -- i.e. the parametron fabric is decisively useful on a nonlinear-memory task, the regime where NARMA10 (Batch 4) hid any advantage.

eval_regime: temporal-XOR(D1=2,D2=4) on coupled sub-threshold parametron reservoir envelope model (sim_reservoir_boundary.py); ridge readout to +/-1; hyperparameters selected on VAL accuracy and reported on held-out TEST; 5 seeds; linear delay-line and tanh ESN baselines
question: [B5-control] On the same temporal-XOR task, a trained linear delay-line readout stays below 60% held-out TEST accuracy (near the 50% chance level), confirming the task is genuinely nonlinear and not solvable by a linear filter.

eval_regime: temporal-XOR(D1=2,D2=4) on coupled sub-threshold parametron reservoir envelope model (sim_reservoir_boundary.py); ridge readout to +/-1; hyperparameters selected on VAL accuracy and reported on held-out TEST; 5 seeds; linear delay-line and tanh ESN baselines
note: reservoir test acc 0.676 vs linear 0.476 (margin +0.201; ESN 0.792) -- HIT

eval_regime: temporal-XOR(D1=2,D2=4) on coupled sub-threshold parametron reservoir envelope model (sim_reservoir_boundary.py); ridge readout to +/-1; hyperparameters selected on VAL accuracy and reported on held-out TEST; 5 seeds; linear delay-line and tanh ESN baselines

temporal-XOR under Gaussian readout-feature noise (feature-std units) on the coupled sub-threshold parametron reservoir (sim_reservoir_noise.py); hyperparameters frozen at clean(sigma=0) VAL, readout retrained per noise level, noise on train+test applied identically to reservoir and linear delay-line; 5 seeds; margin = reservoir_test_acc - linear_test_acc
question: [B6] The parametron reservoir's temporal-XOR advantage over a trained linear delay-line SURVIVES moderate readout noise: at Gaussian feature noise sigma=0.3 (feature-std units) the margin (reservoir_test_acc - linear_test_acc) is still at least 0.10.

eval_regime: temporal-XOR under Gaussian readout-feature noise (feature-std units) on the coupled sub-threshold parametron reservoir (sim_reservoir_noise.py); hyperparameters frozen at clean(sigma=0) VAL, readout retrained per noise level, noise on train+test applied identically to reservoir and linear delay-line; 5 seeds; margin = reservoir_test_acc - linear_test_acc
question: [B6-boundary] The advantage has a noise ceiling: at heavy readout noise sigma=1.0 the margin (reservoir_test_acc - linear_test_acc) falls below 0.10, i.e. the parametron reservoir's nonlinear-memory edge is not noise-proof and degrades to near-parity under heavy noise.
